paper_title: Chromatographic Analysis and Infrared Irradiation Drying Impact on Bee Pollen

methods.description: Chromatographic analyses were conducted using HPLC/DAD to analyze fresh and dried pollen extracts for phenolic constituents. The method involves separation using a specific column and gradient, with spectral data collection and analysis.

steps.description: 20 µL of fresh pollen extract and 10 µL of dried bee pollen extract were analyzed in a Gilson 170.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and load pollen extracts into HPLC/DAD system

steps.description: Separation was attained with a Waters Spherisorb ODS2 (5 mm) column (4.6 × 250 mm) by an acidified water-acetonitrile gradient with a flow rate of 0.8 mL/min, and a column temperature of 24°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform chromatographic separation using specified column and gradient

steps.description: Standard chromatograms were plotted at λ max 260 and 340 nm.
Spectral data for all peaks were accumulated in the range 220-400 nm using DAD (Gilson 170) and further analyzed with the software Unipoint.
steps.searchable_summary: Accumulate and analyze spectral data using Unipoint software

steps.description: All the extracts were submitted to a qualitative and quantitative analysis of the main phenolic constituents.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform qualitative and quantitative analysis of phenolic constituents

steps.description: Structural determination of the phenolic compounds was performed according to the theoretical rules presented in Campos and Markham and by comparison with our internal spectral database.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine structure of phenolic compounds using theoretical rules and spectral database

steps.description: Concentrations were determined using the following standard curve equation obtained with rutin: y = 4.2159 × 10 −9 A + 0.0062 R 2 = 0.9996.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate concentrations using standard curve equation
